records.risk: retry/replay can duplicate or corrupt side effects
records.mitigation: add idempotency key, transaction boundary, or output existence check

records.risk: execution can hang, drift, or fail due to external service
records.mitigation: wrap transport with timeout, fake transport tests, and normalized failure packets

records.risk: child process can hang or leak output/state
records.mitigation: use process boundary with timeout, captured stdout/stderr, and exit-code contract

records.risk: state can leak across requests and make replay non-deterministic
records.mitigation: checkpoint state or isolate it behind a state adapter

records.risk: input format changes can silently break extraction
records.mitigation: store raw input samples and negative parser fixtures

records.risk: retry loop can amplify failures and cost
records.mitigation: bound attempts and convert exhaustion into InterruptPacket

records.risk: secrets can leak into logs, reports, or replay artifacts
records.mitigation: redact before persistence and test redaction policy

records.risk: insufficient cache key can mix users/providers/options
records.mitigation: include identity, provider, model, schema version, and policy inputs in cache key

records.risk: runtime behavior depends on ambient machine state
records.mitigation: capture effective config and validate required fields

records.risk: time-dependent logic can be unreproducible
records.mitigation: inject clock and record schedule/timezone in artifacts

records.risk: file operations may escape intended workspace
records.mitigation: resolve paths and enforce workspace allowlist

records.risk: large artifacts slow scans and hide important source
records.mitigation: store manifest/hash and avoid loading large binary content

records.risk: untyped payloads allow contract drift
records.mitigation: introduce TypedDict/Pydantic/JSON Schema at boundary

records.risk: different failures collapse into unclear runtime bugs
records.mitigation: map exceptions to stable error classes and retry policy

records.risk: tests become flaky, expensive, and hard to reproduce
records.mitigation: use fake provider transport for default verification

records.risk: bad job can retry forever or block the queue
records.mitigation: add max attempts, quarantine, and dead-letter handling

records.risk: partial writes can leave inconsistent state
records.mitigation: make transaction scope explicit and test rollback

records.risk: system can exceed provider quotas
records.mitigation: record provider quota state and route through rate-limit policy

records.risk: protocol-specific errors leak into business logic
records.mitigation: normalize protocol failures at adapter boundary

records.risk: untrusted template/context can inject unsafe output
records.mitigation: explicit escaping/trust policy and negative tests

records.risk: auth failures can be confused with runtime bugs
records.mitigation: return AuthDecision with allow/deny/error reasons

records.risk: unbounded labels can break observability backend
records.mitigation: apply cardinality guard and redaction

records.risk: floating outputs fail nondeterministically
records.mitigation: declare tolerance, seed, backend, and shape/dtype contract

records.risk: generator changes can silently alter many artifacts
records.mitigation: golden-output tests and generated artifact manifest

records.risk: registry metadata can diverge from plugin source
records.mitigation: verify source hash and mark degraded on mismatch

records.risk: fallback path may violate original contract
records.mitigation: validate fallback output against same contract

records.risk: LLM context overflow can truncate critical evidence
records.mitigation: compact facts and retry with evidence-only context

records.risk: implementation can optimize for the wrong objective
records.mitigation: run Prompt Adequacy Gate and emit ClarificationPacket

records.risk: automated code edits can damage user source
records.mitigation: use sandbox PatchPackage and require human approval before source mutation

records.risk: context-only files can dominate core analysis
records.mitigation: apply source_scope_policy and prefer active core paths

records.risk: dependency changes can break plugin execution
records.mitigation: mark capability degraded and trigger on-demand repair path

records.risk: entrypoint mixes validation, decisions, I/O, and rendering
records.mitigation: thin route, extract service/use-case, and contract test response shape

records.risk: execution cannot be reproduced after failure
records.mitigation: persist raw input, normalized output, config, version, and report artifacts

records.risk: adapters return incompatible shapes
records.mitigation: validate adapter output against shared result schema

records.risk: human-readable report may include unsupported judgments
records.mitigation: separate evidence-backed facts from judgments and cite sources
